title: Eroteum macrophyllum
taxonomy: Eroteum macrophyllum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It belongs to the genus Eroteum, which is a monotypic genus. Eroteum macrophyllum is native to Borneo and is a rare and endangered species. Further classification of the species includes its placement in the order Malpighiales. Its classification as a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae indicates its relationship to other plants within this family.
uses: Eroteum macrophyllum has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its leaves and roots believed to possess various medicinal properties. The plant's roots are said to be used to treat fever and rheumatism, while its leaves are used to treat skin conditions and wounds. More studies are needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
